006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

017package org.apache.juneau.commons.inject;
018
019import static java.lang.annotation.ElementType.*;
020import static java.lang.annotation.RetentionPolicy.*;
021
022import java.lang.annotation.*;
023
024/**
025 * Bean injection annotation.
026 *
027 * <p>
028 * Used on methods and fields of {@link BeanStore}-managed objects to denote methods and fields that override and
029 * customize beans used by the framework.
030 *
031 * <h5 class='figure'>Example</h5>
032 * <p class='bcode'>
033 *    <jc>// Rest resource that uses a customized call logger.</jc>
034 *    <ja>@Rest</ja>
035 *    <jk>public class</jk> MyRest <jk>extends</jk> BasicRestServlet {
036 *
037 *       <jc>// Option #1:  As a field.</jc>
038 *       <ja>@Bean</ja>
039 *       CallLogger <jf>myCallLogger</jf> = CallLogger.<jsm>create</jsm>().logger(<js>"mylogger"</js>).build();
040 *
041 *       <jc>// Option #2:  As a method.</jc>
042 *       <ja>@Bean</ja>
043 *       <jk>public</jk> CallLogger myCallLogger() {
044 *          <jk>return</jk> CallLogger.<jsm>create</jsm>().logger(<js>"mylogger"</js>).build();
045 *       }
046 *    }
047 * </p>
048 *
049 * <p>
050 *    The {@link Bean#name()}/{@link Bean#value()} attributes are used to differentiate between named beans.
051 * </p>
052 * <h5 class='figure'>Example</h5>
053 * <p class='bcode'>
054 *    <jc>// Customized default request headers.</jc>
055 *    <ja>@Bean</ja>(<js>"defaultRequestHeaders"</js>)
056 *    HeaderList <jf>defaultRequestHeaders</jf> = HeaderList.<jsm>create</jsm>().set(ContentType.<jsf>TEXT_PLAIN</jsf>).build();
057 *
058 *    <jc>// Customized default response headers.</jc>
059 *    <ja>@Bean</ja>(<js>"defaultResponseHeaders"</js>)
060 *    HeaderList <jf>defaultResponseHeaders</jf> = HeaderList.<jsm>create</jsm>().set(ContentType.<jsf>TEXT_PLAIN</jsf>).build();
061 * </p>
062 *
063 * <p>
064 *    The {@link Bean#methodScope()} attribute is used to define beans in the scope of specific {@code @RestOp}-annotated methods.
065 * </p>
066 * <h5 class='figure'>Example</h5>
067 * <p class='bcode'>
068 *    <jc>// Set a default header on a specific REST method.</jc>
069 *    <jc>// Input parameter is the default header list builder with all annotations applied.</jc>
070 *    <ja>@Bean</ja>(name=<js>"defaultRequestHeaders"</js>, methodScope=<js>"myRestMethod"</js>)
071 *    <jk>public</jk> HeaderList.Builder myRequestHeaders(HeaderList.Builder <jv>builder</jv>) {
072 *       <jk>return</jk> <jv>builder</jv>.set(ContentType.<jsf>TEXT_PLAIN</jsf>);
073 *    }
074 *
075 *    <jc>// Method that picks up default header defined above.</jc>
076 *    <ja>@RestGet</ja>
077 *    <jk>public</jk> Object myRestMethod(ContentType <jv>contentType</jv>) { ... }
078 * </p>
079 *
080 * <p>
081 *    This annotation can also be used to inject arbitrary beans into the bean store which allows them to be
082 *    passed as resolved parameters on {@code @RestOp}-annotated methods.
083 * </p>
084 * <h5 class='figure'>Example</h5>
085 * <p class='bcode'>
086 *    <jc>// Custom beans injected into the bean store.</jc>
087 *    <ja>@Bean</ja> MyBean <jv>myBean1</jv> = <jk>new</jk> MyBean();
088 *    <ja>@Bean</ja>(<js>"myBean2"</js>) MyBean <jv>myBean2</jv> = <jk>new</jk> MyBean();
089 *
090 *    <jc>// Method that uses injected beans.</jc>
091 *    <ja>@RestGet</ja>
092 *    <jk>public</jk> Object doGet(MyBean <jv>myBean1</jv>, <ja>@Name</ja>(<js>"myBean2"</js>) MyBean <jv>myBean2</jv>) { ... }
093 * </p>
094 *
095 * <p>
096 *    This annotation can also be used on uninitialized fields.  When fields are uninitialized, they will
097 *    be set during initialization based on beans found in the bean store.
098 * </p>
099 * <h5 class='figure'>Example</h5>
100 * <p class='bcode'>
101 *    <jc>// Fields that get set during initialization based on beans found in the bean store.</jc>
102 *    <ja>@Bean</ja> CallLogger <jf>callLogger</jf>;
103 *    <ja>@Bean</ja> BeanStore <jf>beanStore</jf>;  <jc>// Note that the BeanStore itself can be accessed this way.</jc>
104 * </p>
105 *
106 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
107 *    <li class='note'>Methods and fields can be static or non-static.
108 *    <li class='note'>Any injectable beans (including spring beans) can be passed as arguments into methods.
109 *    <li class='note'>Bean names are required when multiple beans of the same type exist in the bean store.
110 *    <li class='note'>By default, the injected bean scope is class-level (applies to the entire class).  The
111 *       {@link Bean#methodScope()} annotation can be used to apply to method-level only (when applicable).
112 * </ul>
113 *
114 * <h5 class='section'>Precedence (since 10.0.0):</h5>
115 * <p>
116 *    {@code @Bean} acts as a <i>programmable default</i>, analogous to Spring's
117 *    <c>&#64;ConditionalOnMissingBean</c>.  When a REST context resolves a framework-managed bean
118 *    (<c>CallLogger</c>, <c>EncoderSet</c>, <c>SerializerSet</c>, <c>ParserSet</c>, <c>ThrownStore</c>,
119 *    <c>Config</c>, <c>VarResolver</c>, <c>HttpPartSerializer</c>, <c>HttpPartParser</c>, etc.), the lookup
120 *    walks the following tiers in order, returning the first hit:
121 * </p>
122 * <ol>
123 *    <li><b>Overriding-parent bean store</b> — Spring beans (in <c>juneau-rest-server-springboot</c> deployments,
124 *       via <c>SpringBeanStore</c>), or any bean reachable through the configured overriding-parent
125 *       bean-store chain.</li>
126 *    <li><b>{@code @Bean} method/field on the resource class</b> — registered as a regular bean-store
127 *       entry, beating the framework default.</li>
128 *    <li><b>Memoizer-backed framework default</b> — built into the context as a default supplier.</li>
129 * </ol>
130 * <p>
131 *    In other words: a Spring <c>&#64;Bean</c> of type <c>CallLogger</c> wins over a {@code @Bean CallLogger}
132 *    method on the same servlet, which in turn wins over the framework's built-in <c>BasicCallLogger</c>.  Non-Spring
133 *    deployments have an empty overriding-parent layer, so the chain naturally collapses to
134 *    {@code @Bean > default}.
135 * </p>
136 * <p>
137 *    Prior to 10.0 the order was {@code @Bean > Spring > default}.  See the 10.0.0 release notes for migration
138 *    guidance if you need to keep that legacy behavior (typically by removing the Spring <c>&#64;Bean</c> or marking the
139 *    {@code @Bean} method's type with a Spring-native override such as <c>&#64;Primary</c>).
140 * </p>
141 *
142 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
143 *    <li class='jc'>{@link BeanStore}
144 *    <li class='jc'>{@link WritableBeanStore}
145 * </ul>
146 */
147@Target({ METHOD, FIELD })
148@Retention(RUNTIME)
149@Inherited
150public @interface Bean {
151
152   /**
153    * Optional description for the exposed API.
154    *
155    * @return The annotation value.
156    * @since 9.2.0
157    */
158   String[] description() default {};
159
160   /**
161    * The short names of the methods that this annotation applies to.
162    *
163    * <p>
164    * Can use <js>"*"</js> to apply to all methods.
165    *
166    * <p>
167    * Ignored for class-level scope.
168    *
169    * @return The short names of the methods that this annotation applies to, or empty if class-scope.
170    */
171   String[] methodScope() default {};
172
173   /**
174    * The bean name to use to distinguish beans of the same type for different purposes.
175    *
176    * <p>
177    * For example, there are two {@code HeaderList} beans:  <js>"defaultRequestHeaders"</js> and <js>"defaultResponseHeaders"</js>.  This annotation
178    * would be used to differentiate between them.
179    *
180    * @return The bean name to use to distinguish beans of the same type for different purposes, or blank if bean type is unique.
181    */
182   String name() default "";
183
184   /**
185    * Same as {@link #name()}.
186    *
187    * @return The bean name to use to distinguish beans of the same type for different purposes, or blank if bean type is unique.
188    */
189   String value() default "";
190
191   /**
192    * Optional ordering value used when exporting ordered bean collections.
193    *
194    * <p>
195    * Lower values are higher precedence.  If both {@link Order} and this value are present on the same
196    * contribution source, {@link Order} wins.
197    *
198    * @return The priority value.
199    */
200   int priority() default Integer.MAX_VALUE / 2;
201}
